heading: Assessment credits for qualifying activities relating to distressed communities
label: 1834a Assessment credits for qualifying activities relating to distressed communities

L. 102–550, § 931(c)“(A) any increase during such period in the amount of new originations of qualified loans and other financial assistance provided for low- and moderate-income persons in distressed communities, or enterprises integrally involved with such neighborhoods, which the Board determines are qualified to be taken into account for purposes of this subsection; and“(B) any increase during such period in the amount of deposits accepted from persons domiciled in the distressed community, at any office of the institution (including any branch) located in any qualified distressed community, and any increase during such period in the amount of new originations of loans and other financial assistance made within that community, except that in no case shall the credit for increased deposits at any institution or branch exceed the credit for increased loan and other financial assistance by the bank or branch in the distressed community.”Subsec. children.heading: Determination of credits for increases in community enterprise activities
children.label: a Determination of credits for increases in community enterprise activities
A
children.text: designate the eligibility requirements for any institution meeting applicable capital standards to receive an assessment credit under ; andsection 1817(b)(7) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: determine the community enterprise assessment credit available to any eligible institution under paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount, during such period, of new originations of qualified loans and other assistance provided for low- and moderate-income persons in distressed communities, or enterprises integrally involved with such neighborhoods, which the Board determines are qualified to be taken into account for purposes of this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: the amount, during such period, of deposits accepted from persons domiciled in the distressed community, at any office of the institution (including any branch) located in any qualified distressed community, and new originations of any loans and other financial assistance made within that community, except that in no case shall the credit for deposits at any institution or branch exceed the credit for loans and other financial assistance by the bank or branch in the distressed community; and

children.label: C
children.text: any increase during the period in the amount of new equity investments in community development financial institutions.

children.label: i
children.text: the amounts of assets described in paragraph (2)(A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amounts of deposits, loans, and other financial assistance described in paragraph (2)(B); and

children.label: i
children.text: any increase during such period in the amount of assets described in paragraph (2)(A) that has been deemed eligible for credit by the Board; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any increase during such period in the amounts of deposits, loans, and other financial assistance described in paragraph (2)(B) that has been deemed eligible for credit by the Board.

children.heading: Determination of qualified loans and other financial assistance
children.label: 4 Determination of qualified loans and other financial assistance
A
children.text: Loans insured or guaranteed by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development, the Secretary of the Department of Veterans Affairs, the Administrator of the Small Business Administration, and the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.label: B
children.text: Loans or financing provided in connection with activities assisted by the Administrator of the Small Business Administration or any small business investment company and investments in small business investment companies.

children.label: C
children.text: Loans or financing provided in connection with any neighborhood housing service program assisted under the Neighborhood Reinvestment Corporation Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 8101

children.label: D
children.text: Loans or financing provided in connection with any activities assisted under the community development block grant program under title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: E
children.text: Loans or financing provided in connection with activities assisted under title II of the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 12721

children.label: F
children.text: Loans or financing provided in connection with a homeownership program assisted under title III of the United States Housing Act of 1937 [ et seq.] or subtitle B or C of title IV of the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act [ et seq., 12891 et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1437aaa42 U.S.C. 12871

children.label: G
children.text: Financial assistance provided through community development corporations.

children.label: H
children.text: Federal and State programs providing interest rate assistance for homeowners.

children.label: I
children.text: Extensions of credit to nonprofit developers or purchasers of low-income housing and small business developments.

children.label: J
children.text: In the case of members of any Federal home loan bank, participation in the community investment fund program established by the Federal home loan banks.

children.label: K
children.text: Conventional mortgages targeted to low- or moderate-income persons.

children.label: i
children.text: commercial facilities that enhance revitalization, community stability, or job creation and retention efforts;

children.label: I
children.text: create or retain jobs for low-income people;

children.label: II
children.text: enhance the availability of products and services to low-income people; or

children.label: III
children.text: create or retain businesses owned by low-income people or residents of a targeted area;

children.label: iii
children.text: community facilities that provide benefits to low-income people or enhance community stability;

children.label: iv
children.text: home ownership opportunities that are affordable to low-income households;

children.label: v
children.text: rental housing that is principally affordable to low-income households; and

children.label: vi
children.text: other activities deemed appropriate by the Board.

children.label: M
children.text: The provision of technical assistance to residents of qualified distressed communities in managing their personal finances through consumer education programs either sponsored or offered by insured depository institutions.

children.label: N
children.text: The provision of technical assistance and consulting services to newly formed small businesses located in qualified distressed communities.

children.label: O
children.text: The provision of technical assistance to, or servicing the loans of low- or moderate-income homeowners and homeowners located in qualified distressed communities.

children.heading: Adjustment of percentage
children.label: 5 Adjustment of percentage
children.text: The Board may increase or decrease the percentage referred to in paragraph (3)(A) for determining the amount of any community enterprise assessment credit pursuant to such paragraph, except that the percentage established for insured depository institutions which meet the community development organization requirements under shall not be less than 3 times the amount of the percentage applicable for insured depository institutions which do not meet such requirements.section 1834b of this title

children.heading: Certain investments not eligible to be taken into account
children.label: 6 Certain investments not eligible to be taken into account
children.text: Loans, financial assistance, and equity investments made by any insured depository institution that are not the result of originations by the institution shall not be taken into account for purposes of determining the amount of any credit pursuant to this subsection.

children.heading: Quantitative analysis of technical assistance
children.label: 7 Quantitative analysis of technical assistance
children.text: The Board may establish guidelines for analyzing the technical assistance described in subparagraphs (M), (N), and (O) of paragraph (4) for the purpose of quantifying the results of such assistance in determining the amount of any community assessment credit under this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: meets the minimum area requirements under paragraph (3) and the eligibility requirements of paragraph (4); and

children.label: B
children.text: is designated as a distressed community by any insured depository institution in accordance with paragraph (2) and such designation is not disapproved under such paragraph.

children.heading: Notice to agency
children.label: i Notice to agency
children.text: Upon designating an area as a qualified distressed community, an insured depository institution shall notify the appropriate Federal banking agency of the designation.

children.heading: Public notice
children.label: ii Public notice
children.text: Upon the effective date of any designation of an area as a qualified distressed community, an insured depository institution shall publish a notice of such designation in major newspapers and other community publications which serve such area.

children.heading: Agency duties relating to designations
children.label: B Agency duties relating to designations

children.heading: Providing information
children.label: i Providing information
children.text: At the request of any insured depository institution, the appropriate Federal banking agency shall provide to the institution appropriate information to assist the institution to identify and designate a qualified distressed community.

children.heading: Period for disapproval
children.label: ii Period for disapproval
children.text: Any notice received by the appropriate Federal banking agency from any insured depository institution under subparagraph (A)(i) shall take effect at the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date such notice is received unless written notice of the approval or disapproval of the application by the agency is provided to the institution before the end of such period.

children.label: A
children.text: the area is within the jurisdiction of 1 unit of general local government;

children.label: B
children.text: the boundary of the area is contiguous; and

children.label: I
children.text: 4,000, if any portion of such area is located within a metropolitan statistical area (as designated by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget) with a population of 50,000 or more; or

children.label: II
children.text: 1,000, in any other case; or

children.label: ii
children.text: is entirely within an Indian reservation (as determined by the Secretary of the Interior).

children.label: A
children.text: At least 30 percent of the residents residing in the area have incomes which are less than the national poverty level.

children.label: B
children.text: The unemployment rate for the area is 1½ times greater than the national average (as determined by the Bureau of Labor Statistics’ most recent figures).

children.label: C
children.text: Such additional eligibility requirements as the Board may, in its discretion, deem necessary to carry out the provisions of this subtitle.

children.heading: Community Enterprise Assessment Credit Board
children.label: d Community Enterprise Assessment Credit Board

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is hereby established the “Community Enterprise Assessment Credit Board”.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury or a designee of the Secretary.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development or a designee of the Secretary.

children.label: C
children.text: The Chairperson of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation or a designee of the Chairperson.

children.label: D
children.text: 2 individuals appointed by the President from among individuals who represent community organizations.

children.heading: Appointed members
children.label: A Appointed members
children.text: Each appointed member shall be appointed for a term of 5 years.

children.heading: Interim appointment
children.label: B Interim appointment
children.text: Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring before the expiration of the term to which such member’s predecessor was appointed shall be appointed only for the remainder of such term.

children.heading: Continuation of service
children.label: C Continuation of service
children.text: Each appointed member may continue to serve after the expiration of the period to which such member was appointed until a successor has been appointed.

children.heading: Chairperson
children.label: 4 Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall serve as the Chairperson of the Board.

children.heading: No pay
children.label: 5 No pay
children.text: No members of the Commission may receive any pay for service on the Board.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: 6 Travel expenses
children.text: Each member shall receive travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in accordance with sections 5702 and 5703 of title 5.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 7 Meetings
children.text: The Board shall meet at the call of the Chairperson or a majority of the Board’s members.

children.heading: Procedure for determining community enterprise assessment credits
children.label: 1 Procedure for determining community enterprise assessment credits
children.text: The Board shall establish procedures for accepting and considering applications by insured depository institutions under subsection (a)(1) for community enterprise assessment credits and making determinations with respect to such applications.

children.heading: Notice to FDIC
children.label: 2 Notice to FDIC
children.text: The Board shall notify the applicant and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation of any determination of the Board with respect to any application referred to in paragraph (1) in sufficient time for the Corporation to include the amount of such credit in the computation of the semiannual assessment to which such credit is applicable.

children.heading: Availability of funds
children.label: f Availability of funds
children.text: The provisions of this section shall not take effect until appropriations are specifically provided in advance. There are hereby authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.heading: Prohibition on double funding for same activities
children.label: g Prohibition on double funding for same activities

children.text: has received assistance within the preceding 12-month period, or has an application for assistance pending, under ; orsection 4704 of this title

has ever received assistance, under , for the same activity during the same semiannual period for which the institution seeks a community enterprise assessment credit under this section.section 4707 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the amount of funds appropriated for purposes of carrying out this section for any fiscal year are insufficient to award the amount of assessment credits for which insured depository institutions have applied and are eligible under this section, the Board shall, in awarding community enterprise assessment credits for qualifying activities under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (a)(2) for any semiannual period for which such appropriation is available, determine which institutions shall receive an award.

children.heading: Priority for support of efforts of CDFI
children.label: B Priority for support of efforts of CDFI
children.text: The Board shall give priority to institutions that have supported the efforts of community development financial institutions in the qualified distressed community.

children.heading: Degree of difficulty
children.label: i Degree of difficulty
children.text: The degree of difficulty in carrying out the activities that form the basis for the institution’s application.

children.heading: Community impact
children.label: ii Community impact
children.text: The extent to which the activities that form the basis for the institution’s application have benefited the qualified distressed community.

children.heading: Innovation
children.label: iii Innovation
children.text: The degree to which the activities that form the basis for the institution’s application have incorporated innovative methods for meeting community needs.

children.heading: Leverage
children.label: iv Leverage
children.text: The leverage ratio between the dollar amount of the activities that form the basis for the institution’s application and the amount of the assessment credit calculated in accordance with this section for such activities.

children.heading: Size
children.label: v Size
children.text: The amount of total assets of the institution.

children.heading: New entry
children.label: vi New entry
children.text: Whether the institution had provided financial services in the designated distressed community before such semiannual period.

children.heading: Need for subsidy
children.label: vii Need for subsidy
children.text: The degree to which the qualified activity which forms the basis for the application needs enhancement through an assessment credit.

children.heading: Extent of distress in community
children.label: viii Extent of distress in community
children.text: The degree of poverty and unemployment in the designated distressed community, the proportion of the total population of the community which are low-income families and unrelated individuals, and the extent of other adverse economic conditions in such community.

children.heading: Qualifying investments
children.label: 2 Qualifying investments
children.text: If the amount of funds appropriated for purposes of carrying out this section for any fiscal year are insufficient to award the amount of assessment credits for which insured depository institutions have applied and are eligible under this section, the Board shall, in awarding community enterprise assessment credits for qualifying activities under subsection (a)(2)(C) for any semiannual period for which such appropriation is available, determine which institutions shall receive an award based on the leverage ratio between the dollar amount of the activities that form the basis for the institution’s application and the amount of the assessment credit calculated in accordance with this section for such activities.

children.heading: Determination of amount of assessment credit
children.label: i Determination of amount of assessment credit
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, the determination of the amount of any community enterprise assessment credit under subsection (a)(3) for any insured depository institution for any semiannual period shall be made solely at the discretion of the Board. No insured depository institution shall be awarded community enterprise assessment credits for any semiannual period in excess of an amount determined by the Board.

children.heading: Appropriate Federal banking agency
children.label: 1 Appropriate Federal banking agency
children.text: The term “appropriate Federal banking agency” has the meaning given to such term in .section 1813(q) of this title

children.heading: Board
children.label: 2 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Community Enterprise Assessment Credit Board established under the amendment made by subsection (d).2So in original. The words “under the amendment made” probably should not appear.2

children.heading: Insured depository institution
children.label: 3 Insured depository institution
children.text: The term “insured depository institution” has the meaning given to such term in .section 1813(c)(2) of this title

children.heading: Community development financial institution
children.label: 4 Community development financial institution
children.text: The term “community development financial institution” has the same meaning as in .section 4702(5) of this title

children.heading: Affiliate
children.label: 5 Affiliate
children.text: The term “affiliate” has the same meaning as in .section 1841 of this title
